sentences.text: Good morning!
Y'all hear me?
Oh, we'll stop.
-Oh.
Connecting to audio, slowly but surely.
Good morning.
Can you hear me, Shelby?
I can hear ya!
See if I get my right camera here.
There we go.
Hello, hello!
How are you?
Doing good.
Nice to meet you.
Good!
Yes, so nice to meet you as well!
Hi, Mike!
Hi, how are you?
I am doing alright.
You know, Wednesday, I always have a little pep in my step.
We can see the finish line, so….
hump day, right?

I always… it's funny, because my daughter's boyfriend, he graduated from college this year, so he joined the working world, or whatever, and you know, and I would always tease him, and it's like, because she'll talk to him at night, or whatever.
It's like, well, you made it through Tuesdays, it's almost hump day, and I said.
battle.
You only got 45 more years of this, buddy.
I was like, you're in good… you know what I mean?
Oh my god!
He's like, will you please just, you know, he's like, you're making me depressed.
That is so funny.
Yeah, my dad will give me a hard time, too.
I mean, I'm 10 years in the workforce, and he'll… I'm like, how have you been doing this?
Because he's a few years from retirement.
I'm like, you guys, you've been doing this?
for decades now?
And he's like, yes, Shelby, this is called working.
I'm like, who signed us up for this?
Yo.
Yeah, who's… who came up with this scheme?
Yeah.
Right, which, you know, I could, … I mean, what is it?
It was Ford, right?
Wasn't it Ford back in the day?
Me too.
Yeah, for sure.
Oh yeah, I feel like we could… I feel like we could tighten in those work hours a little bit, but anyway, I digress.
Thank you guys so much for taking the time to chat this morning.
I know, Mike, you were on the AI Lab call with my director, Patrick, is that correct?
Yep, that's where I saw you guys.
And I know, too, that our prior marketing director, she had reached out to you guys for a couple of the properties where we had stopped using….
brown.
or Sprout, yes.
Awesome.
Just for… for presents and some other things, but, … … I was more impressed, actually, after I saw it, and what you guys did and everything else.
I thought, oh, you know, we should probably step back and take a look at this.
Yeah.
coming on board here, I wanted to make sure that he got an opportunity to kind of see what you guys They're able to joke.
Yeah, absolutely.
Thank you so much for reaching out, and yeah, I'm excited to show y'all.

I'd met with Mandy a few times, and … so yeah, it sounds like… and I have a little bit of background, you know, y'all use Sprout Social, And it sounds like it's a little bit more hands-on than… I… ideally, y'all would have, right?
Is that correct?
So my pulse on… I've been with the company, this is my, I don't know, third week.
It seems like a decent balance right now with, like, guardrails, you know, post-approvals and things like that.
but from what I gather from Rachel, is that it's pretty encompassing besides the social product.
We really do like what it does for, review, monitoring.
That's a big piece of it.
Okay.
So I know she really likes that as well.
Ultimately.
So, review, monitoring, and you'll have to forgive me, I don't know that Mandy and I had spoken about that.
We kind of focused on Google, and then social, because we actually do have a new automated tool with the social piece as well.
But in regards to the review management, what is Sprout Social's process currently?

So we don't use Sprout, we have Sochi, and Sochi has a direct connect, I think, with all of our… you know, the review sites, the ILS sites, it's a really clean interface, you know, AI kind of gen responses, and I don't see a lot of holes in that right now.
Okay.
And then the social media side, I think it's kind of like a….
a B-minus right now with the content that it produces, but not hearing any complaints thus far on scheduling, things like that.
It seems pretty smart, but just the richness of the content maybe needs room for improvement.
Okay, okay, perfect.

And going back to, reputation really quickly, because one thing that's going to be a huge differentiator from us versus Sochi is we're going to direct… or directly integrate with Entrata, because right now, with Sochi, as renters move in and move out, y'all have to manually make sure that those contacts are updated, is that correct?
Correct.
As far as, like, the… their information in Trada?
So, Sochi does not integrate with… in Entrata.
I don't believe so, so the way… the way I understand it is Entrada talks to the third-party sites, like the ILS, you know, Apartments.
com, apartment ratings, and all that, and Sochi talks to that, or, like, listens to that.
Okay, perfect, which, yeah, we'll do the same thing, but then, yeah, as far as, like, review garnering, you can directly set up, like, trigger events with our, dashboard.
So I… I will show y'all that here in a second.
I know that I'm getting ahead of myself.
And actually, if y'all would just prefer to focus on Google for the hour, we can absolutely do that.
Would y'all prefer to kind of… Focus on Google for today.
Not necessarily, because I thought it was interesting when we saw it.

What interested me was, you know, that it did do kind of like the Craigslist, it did some of the, you know, it automated a lot through the AI, it automated some of these tasks that we were missing on.
Okay.

I always thought that it's been a weak point for us, because we're a smaller company, you know, it's Bob, and it's an assistant, and trying to get them to actually update their, excuse me, Craigslist things, like their Facebook Marketplace, those types of things, it just doesn't get done unless we're, like, banging on people, or it's Bob Marketing Department having to actually go out and do it, and it just… it gets old.
Yep, nope, absolutely.
I thought it was interesting that, as well as, like, the Google portion of it, answering questions.
So, if you can touch on that, that'd be great as well, Shelby.
Okay, absolutely.
All right, well then, yeah, I'll touch on a few things today.
And then as far as AI, you know, Mike, I know, of course, you joined that AI Lab call.
Is there anything in particular that has you kind of focused on AI right now?
… Yeah, I mean, I think everybody in the industry right now is looking at the… there's products coming down the day.
I think I get an outreach from somebody with some kind of new… And again, we are, we're small, so we're generally followers, we're not leaders.
We wait until somebody else has done it, and I see that it works, and it's proven, before we kind of, like.
follow them down the rabbit hole.
So we're using some AI products.
I mean, we are using Elise AI, for, like, our leasing.

We are using RealPage's AI revenue management tool, but those are kind of like the big ones right now, but obviously we realize that there are other avenues out there that we need to start exploring, just so that we're keeping up.
And this is one of them.
Okay, got it.
Thank you so much.
So… as far as the amount of properties in the portfolio, there are 14 that y'all handle marketing for, is that still correct?
I think it I think it's, like, 16 or 17, I gotta double check.
Okay.
Okay, got it.
And then, … Y'all use Sochi for reputation management, y'all use Sprout Social for a mix of both Meta and Google, is that correct?
No more Sprout.
Nope.
So she's taking that on.
Yep.
We cut… so we used Sochi for a long time, then we had a ton of problems.
They went through, a lot of internal changes, our sales reps all got cut, and service went… It just went sideways for, like, 6 months, and then when a contract came up, we couldn't go back with them.
Okay.
Sprout for a year.
We were not happy with Sprout, and we went back to Sochi.
Okay, got it.
So, that Sochi contract, is that up?
Soon, or did y'all just sign for a year?
I think it's just… I think it's a May timeframe, if I don't… if I remember.
find one here, social media… looks like end of June.
End of June, alright.
Okay, so I guess, yeah, I'm of course happy to show y'all our social and reputation, but it sounds like y'all are probably pretty locked in for a while with that tool, okay?
Yeah, is the Craigslist stuff that Mike was talking about, is that tied into your social tool, or is that, like, a different listing feature?

So, so we do not help with Craigslist or Facebook Marketplace, so when it comes to what we would be doing on social, similarly to how we create and, automate content on Google, we do the same thing with your Facebook profiles.
We're gonna, of course, differentiate content, because we know that Google does want to see different content across different platforms.
But yes, as far as, like, Facebook Marketplace or Craigslist, Facebook Marketplace doesn't allow third-party vendors, if I'm mistaken, anymore.
Yep.
Yeah, which is a shame, because we used to do that, and people really liked that, but… okay, lovely.
Well then, I am gonna hop right in.
I will show you how this works.
Mike, I am unsure if you are aware that Mandy had just rolled out our Google tool on 4 properties, so… oh, you did mention, yes, because you wanted to loop in.
Yeah, yeah, right.
And then that's what I said, I was like, oh, this is so much more than that, and it was….
So, yep.
Okay.
Alright, perfect.
Well then, I will hop right in.
Show you how this works.
Alright.

So, when it comes to marketing, especially with multifamily, you know, in the marketing world, you're not focused on, you know, one profile or one thing, you're focused on multiple, 16, 17, so… We have created a product suite, specific to multifamily, so that's gonna be, you know, a huge differentiator for us, because we understand, what those renters want to see, and how to really make y'all's lives easier as well with integrations.
But, our bread and butter, kind of where we started, was our Google Business Profile automation tool.
So, Google still dominates search.
92% of searches on the web are done starting on Google, with about 87% of renters starting their search for a new apartment on Google as well.

So, when they come across your Google Business Profiles, we of course want to make sure that y'all look as good as possible, that these renters are able to gather as much information on your property just by looking at the profile.
But beyond, digital curb appeal, this is where that local SEO piece comes in.

So, Google rewards relevant, frequent, and unique content to your profiles with an increase in local rank on Google Maps, KPIs like property website traffic, impressions, of course, so on and so forth.
So, while what we're doing, of course, does make sure that, we're standardizing your brand online, it additionally, what we see in regard to increase in SEO over time is pretty phenomenal.
So, the reason why we have created this product suite is we hear more and more about diminishing manpower.
Marketing teams are being asked for stronger, results, oftentimes with fewer resources.
On-site teams sometimes are tasked with some things, and, you know, they're overburdened as well, and then ultimately, the renter experience is not standardized.
So, with that being said, here's our Google Business AI tool.
So, the way that it works is we're going to integrate with Entrada.
That is, Entrada is who y'all use, right?
I know that y'all said RealPage for revenue management, okay.
So, we integrate with Entrada to do 4 things on the profiles daily.
So, first and foremost, we're going to use your photos that live in Entrada to create property and amenity-rich content daily.
We'll pull over your available floor plans with the starting price.
I know that this was really important to Mandy, so we make sure that that's reflected properly on Google.
We help manage your Q&A feed, and then anytime y'all offer concessions and specials, and that's entered in the field in Entrata, we will, create a post about that as well.
Any questions before I hop in and kind of show you what this looks like?
Floor plan images, all that kind of stuff that's pulled from Entrada.
That's gonna pull right from Entrada, absolutely.
Gotcha.
Do you do, like, the specials feature?
Does Google still offer that as, like, a special offer, or does it just come through as a post when we do the concessions?
So, you know, I've kind of seen it… pull up in, like, yesterday I was looking at this profile.
And the way that I had seen it, because, you know, Google's always kind of testing things.
They change stuff every day, don't tell you.
They love to change things, and I live in Austin, Texas, and I see those Waymos drive past my house every day.
Hey, I get it, Alphabet is a huge company.
Where is their support team?
I… I don't understand.
… That's funny.
Yeah.
So….
shelter.
This is… this is how it's been showing up right here.
Okay, that is in the offer format.
Cool.
Okay, awesome.

It's an offer format, and while we're looking at this, anytime y'all do offer a concession or special, we're gonna create a post about it once a week while the, concession is running, and then once the concession expires, we're actually going to delete the post or the offer, just to prevent anyone from coming in and out date, res… sorry.
Referencing an outdated special.
Very cool.
Alright, so, … Yeah, here is Sherwood Towers currently.
So, just to give you an idea of kind of what our content looks like, again, we're gonna use y'all's photos, and then, we leverage AI to focus on any amenities that are being shown.

So, we really try to paint a picture for the prospective resident on what it's like to live at the property, so… Unwind under the shade awning, take in city views, so we do want to make sure that it is… it looks a little bit more personable than, you know, some of those other vendors out there.
Yeah, are there any settings and stuff that we can set for, like, hey… use these kind of phrases and stuff to help it, you know, maybe get, like, SEO keywords, things like that in there.
Absolutely, yes.

So, in the event that there are any SEO keywords that you want to focus on, or brand guidelines involved, it might make sense for you and I to actually get on a call with your customer success manager sometime soon, because we understand that brand guidelines are important.
So we can align on that, make sure that we're using the verbiage that you want to reflect, so on and so forth.
Okay, great.
Yeah.
That's what… I think I need, access still to the interface.
I'm not sure I'm in there yet.
Yes, that is important, huh?
We should get that.
So, yes, let me make a note of that afterwards.
Okay… login.
All right!
Any questions on these posts?
Do you… is there anything else that takes place with the profile itself, like shuffling, the actual uploaded photos, like, by the manager?
Like, if we do a new photo to Entrada.
Do you guys post to the actual photos section, or just, like, the posts section?
So these are gonna be the posts.
Okay.
As far as any of these photos, these… this will still kind of be in your wheelhouse, Bob.
So, yes, if there are any photos that you do want to change directly on Google, that would… that would kind of, be up to you.
Gotcha.
And then, like, the reviews, everything associated with that is not with this product, this is just kind of, like, the postings, you're saying?
Yep, exactly.
Gotcha.
Yep, alright.
So… with that product's feed.
So, again, we are going to pull over your available floor plans with the starting price once a day.

If you click into the floor plan, it's going to give you a little bit more in-depth look of what that floor plan entails, and then we're ultimately going to be driving the property website traffic back to your landing pages.
So if we click on this.
it'll take us right to Sherwood Towers, property website.
Nice.
Yup!
And it really is, you know, especially….
Shelby.
Oh, no.
My number is wrong.
Sorry, what.
That's probably, like, a… that's probably a, … I was looking at the phone number.
And that's probably one of the, … what do they call it?
The, ….
like, directory listings?
Are y'all.
Yeah, or something, one of, like, the… through and trot on the back end, they have phone numbers and stuff, so they can track where the source is.
Oh, yes, okay, so….
off guard.
So, we actually don't do anything with this.
We can, on that point, though, Mike, we can use UTM links, if y'all did want to, kind of.
hone in even further on the, what the traffic will look like from this tool.
So, if that is something that y'all do have, and y'all were interested in rolling that out, and Bob, we can talk about that more with your, Customer Success Manager.
and use those UTM links as well.
Yeah, that would be good.
I'm trying to find out if Entrada can read those.
I think they can, but they're not positive yet.
Okay.
But yeah, it looks like it goes to a booking portal.
I'm not sure… I gotta make sure that's in our GA4 as well, but that's… that's good, so we can put custom, like, UTMs in for the different, products and that.
Yep, absolutely.
Awesome.
Alright, so yes, this listing's information we will not touch, just so you know.
But yeah, so products feed, that's all us.
Posts are gonna be us.
And then the last piece that is going to be asked will be the Q&A feed.
So, what we've done here is we saw, oftentimes, a question would be asked, and it would either never be answered, or be answered, you know, way later than the question was asked.
So, to get ahead of a prospective renter even needing to ask a question.
We place up to 20 questions on the profile based on what your property offers, and we edit that answer daily to reflect any changes.
So these questions are gonna be about availability.
Pet policy, amenities, … I'm trying to think if there's anything else.
Okay, so if we… and we can work on this as well, Bob, if there are any, like, if y'all have EV chargers or anything like that, that's something that we can kind of focus on a little bit more.
But yes, this will not only, of course, ideally answer any prospective residents' questions, but we're signaling to Google that y'all are a super active business.

So, for example, product and availability, those answers could change daily, but on-site laundry, it's not likely that, you know, one day you'll have on-site laundry and the next you won't, but in editing that answer daily, we're again signaling to Google, y'all are a super active business to make sure that we are capitalizing on that organic SEO.
Could you remind me, is this, like, a… Do you format this on the business back end, both the question and the answer, or is this, like, a public submission for the questions?
So, so we… this is gonna be on the back end.
Okay, gotcha, thanks.
Yeah, absolutely.
All right, and that concludes my Google Business spiel, which I'll have on the four properties currently.
And I lost my email that has those four.
Oh, I've got it in my notes here.
I think it was Brittany, Chalfont, Sherwood, and Liv Southside.
Okay, thank you.
Yup.
Yep.
That's pretty slick, so it's just, like, $150 a profile flat rate.
Yeah, absolutely.
I believe I offered, $140 for the full portfolio, so a little bit extra discount there.
But yes, that is… that's the price that… down from $200, so y'all do have a discounted, rate in place as well.
Nice.
Do you have anything, like, in your notes on our account?
Maybe what, you know, the strategy was Mandy was thinking when she picked those properties?
You know… What I'm thinking, and I'll have to dig a little deeper.
Okay.
I did try to gather as much info from the last call.
Is it possible that Sprout Social was not being used on that… those four?
Yeah, I know there's… I know there's some too, Mike, that we were not on.
Sochi for review monitoring, but this wouldn't have covered that one.
Okay, I'll try and get that answer for you, as far as strategy goes.
I know the biggest thing was, she really just wanted to kind of get a test sample.
Yeah.
Yeah, got it.
So, yes, we are, you know, gathering that data on our end as far as the increase in KPIs.
feeding Google organically is a little bit more of a long play, so we do start to see those KPIs kind of increase at the 6-9 month mark.
We do technically operate on annual agreements, but Mandy, was able to get a 6-month agreement in place.
So, yes, ideally, we will start to see those KPIs lift at that 6-month mark, but yeah, typically.
6 to 9 months is the sweet spot.
I'm sorry, I was looking at the contract.
Can you say that again?
Like, you start to see, like, KPIs evolved at that point in time, you're thinking?
Yeah, that's where we really, you know, undeniably see a lift.
What kind of metrics do you, you know, do clients typically see?
What are you, like, aiming for?

Yeah, so it kind of does vary, but, just so y'all know, we do work with, you know, the Camden's of the world, Asset Living, S2, so Camden's had us in place, now for, I want to say, 3 or 4 years, and what they did is they trialed us while trying to optimize their profiles in-house as well.
And yeah, the KPIs that they saw, in addition to the time savings, was pretty undeniable.
But I can send over some, like, Pittsburgh-specific KPI reports for you after the call.
Oh, that'd be great, yeah, okay.
What that looks like.
Yeah, do you… do you think if I… I… I just know that, like, … Mike, would you say yards in Eastside are, like, a Class A property?
us.
If we wanted to tag, like, a Class A property, could we get it in on that 6-month, just to see, like, what kind of lift we could do, like a optimized versus, you know, other plan?
Yeah, you said yards, and what was the other one?
Yards and Eastside, those ones stand out, just as… you know, I might pick one of those to see, you know, what kind of lift.
I think those… they get a ton of inquiries, they probably have really active Google profiles.
Okay.
I'm very curious, you know, maybe what the performance would be on one of those.
Okay, yeah, I mean, I… I don't… y'all already… y'all already got that 6 months, so I want to say that I should be able to honor that.
So, yes, I will check with Patrick after this call, just get… get an okay from him, but yeah, I want to say that we should be able to offer that, since y'all already have that contract in place.
Okay, yeah, that's kind of why I was asking, I was like, hmm, we don't… we're missing a property class maybe on there, okay.
Yeah, yeah, absolutely.
And something to note is it works on all property classes, it really does.
We've seen a lot of success even with, you know, affordable portfolios.
But y'all have some student properties as well, is that correct?
Okay.
Yeah, 100%.
Cause yeah, there are a few things that we can do there as well, like, do y'all, offer those… not units, but by… by bedroom?
Yeah, by the bed leasing.
Okay, because we can actually break down that on the products feed side.
Hmm.
So something to note.

We can also, going back to the brand guidelines, you know, steps away from campus, focus on, you know, any… if y'all have, like, study rooms, something like that, we can really kind of tweak the verbiage, to gear it a little bit more towards student renters.
Very cool.
Yeah!
Okay, so I know that we did establish that Sochi's in place until next June.
Do y'all have any interest in seeing social or reputation real quick?
You go on to… I'll watch for sure.
I don't know if you have time, Mike.
If you don't mind, I think I'd probably just leave it with you, Bob.
That sounds good, yeah.
Yep, you got it.
Mike, did you have any questions?
Does this….
I thought that was pretty good.
It's exactly what I wanted to see, and just, you know, I appreciate the time today, and I'll leave you to Bob.
Thank you very much, Shelby.
Yeah, thank you, Mike.
It was lovely to meet you.
Have a good day.
Thanks, Mike.
Alright!
Yeah, I'm always an open eye and an open ear.
There's so much stuff coming out, and I want to make sure we've got the best for all… all….
Checkbox.
I know, I'm so bummed.
I had it marked in my calendar.
I was like, okay, Sprout Social's up, I thought it was end of year, and yeah, Bob, I was gonna find ya, and I was gonna pitch on.
Well, there's some… there's some properties that aren't there still.
I think they might be, like, the… suburban, lower… lower tier, lower expense ones, but yeah, we do have a couple properties not on Sochi still.
Okay, okay, got it.
Well then… Yes, we've recently brought a few people over from Sochi recently.
They had come from multifamily, they went to Sochi, and they actually moved over to us because the integration is so different.
You know, not knocking Sochi, Sochi's great, but when it comes to multifamily, we have a little bit more of an advantage.
… So with that being said, on the social AI front, this is a relatively new tool to us, but, we've been getting some great feedback.
We've had it in place for a year now, but we recently rolled out a dashboard that our, our partners have been loving.
So, similarly to how we create and post content on Google, we're gonna do the same thing on We are going to actually post twice a week on Meta versus daily.
You know, with Google, that frequent, unique content's really important.
With Facebook, these are most likely going to be a lot of, residents who are on here.
So, we post, twice a week, Tuesday, Thursdays.
That's what we see, the highest traffic days.
And yes, we'll use your photos.
We will, speak to both the unit, property, amenities, as well as floor plans.
But I will, and I apologize, we just rolled out a new social deck, and I will be sure to send this over, because our automated content has diversified a little bit as well.
So, we have templated posts now, you know, everyone loves a dog, so… about, like, a pet policy thing, and having a dog.

I included holiday posts, that's something that were automated, as well, but… We also understand, kind of going back to social media being resident-facing, y'all are going to have community events, most likely, you know, maybe a movie night, you have food trucks on the property.
So, you would also get access to this dashboard with the tool.
You would have, both your Google and, Meta posts in here.
A few different ways that you can kind of.
use this tool.
So, you can select as many properties as you'd like to see both the published posts on Google and Meta, as well as the scheduled upcoming posts.
For those scheduled posts, you are able to, go in, edit them where you see fit.
If you see a post and you're like, I don't really love this, you can actually regenerate the post entirely automatically.

But then, as far as creating content, you can either select one property, select however many you want, you can select a new post, and you or the team can either Create a post right then and there, or have it scheduled out.
Nice.
So, yes, this has been… really exciting for us, because, yeah, you know, with Facebook and Instagram being more resident-focused, it's exciting to have that option that your team or you can post.
Alright, and that's the social tool.
Any questions on that?
No, no, I like it.
And that's just, you know, I think Soshi does a… an okay job of it, but I know that, Rachel here on the team, she really likes how they do the… kind of all-in-one reputation management, too.
It's, you know, some of the internally focused surveys, some of the externally focused, that's a… that's a big one for us.
Which brings us to….
Alright, drum roll.
And sorry, I've heard you mention Rachel.
Who is Rachel?
She is our marketing and leasing manager.
She's been here, I think, about 4 years now.
Okay.
Worked with Mandy extensively beforehand, and she's on vacation right now, she'd be joining us on the call.
Okay.
But yeah, this is huge, and what Sochi doesn't do is the solicitation for reviews.
That… that irks me.
So, that's gonna be that huge differentiator that I mentioned.
I've… Again.
not to knock Soshi, but I've heard, you know, their onboarding for a lot of their tools is pretty seamless.

I've heard with reputation, it's kind of a pain, which I know y'all are rocking and rolling with it so far, but going back to that review solicitation, that's gonna be the biggest way to increase your star rating, right?
So, that's where the integration with Entrata is really huge, because my understanding is with Sochi, as those contents, or sorry, those, contacts move in and out, you're having to manually do that.
do… what do we do?
We have, … I only know this because I'm really new.

Interesting, okay.
I'm very interested, like, even if we don't have the social tool, we could tack on the, like, the reputation?
Okay.
Yep, so Google is our base tool.
Everything's kind of a la carte, plug and play from there.
Okay, great.
Okay, that's really good to know.
Yeah, absolutely.
So, you know, as things evolve, if, you know, you and I are sitting here this time next year.
